course_title: CI/CD Pipeline in Practice — Automating AWS EKS Deployments with GitLab + Jenkins
description: A hands-on course for developers who want to deploy to Kubernetes (AWS EKS). Rather than diving deep into Kubernetes internals, the focus is on building a GitLab + Jenkins pipeline that automatically deploys your code to EKS. You will set up everything from image build and push, to writing deployment manifests, to kubectl rollouts and rollbacks — constructing a declarative deployment pipeline from scratch.
summary: Starting from a Dockerfile and ECR setup, through a GitLab + Jenkins CI pipeline, Deployment/Service manifest authoring, EKS access configuration, and automated kubectl rollouts from Jenkins — all the way to rolling updates, rollbacks, ConfigMaps, and Secrets for complete Kubernetes deployment automation.
target_audience: Developers who want to deploy to Kubernetes (EKS). Docker basics required; light prior exposure to Kubernetes is sufficient — deep internals knowledge is not needed and can be supplemented with dedicated Kubernetes courses.

lectures.title: EKS Deployment Pipelines — From Push to Kubernetes
Containerizing Your App and Preparing Amazon ECR
Accessing the EKS Cluster and Configuring kubectl
Automated Deployment to EKS from Jenkins
Rolling Updates, Rollbacks, and Health Checks
Managing Manifests and Secrets, and Wrapping Up
